title: Articulation Error Drill Basic
text: Start with one target phoneme and five high-frequency words. Model mouth posture and slow production before child imitation. Track accuracy in blocks of ten trials and move up after stable 80 percent. Add short home practice with the same word set.
do_not: Do not introduce multiple new phonemes in one block.

title: Articulation Error Drill Intermediate
text: Practice target sound in word-initial, medial, and final position. Use model, delayed imitation, then picture naming. Keep each round to twelve items and log repeated error patterns. Roll back to easier cueing when three consecutive errors appear.
do_not: Do not prioritize speed over accuracy.

title: Articulation Error Generalization
text: Transfer accurate production into phrase and short conversation tasks. Use role-play topics such as snack choice and toy request. Give delayed feedback after each turn to build self-monitoring. Recycle high-error contexts in the next session.
do_not: Do not remove feedback entirely during generalization.

title: Phonology Process Intervention Basic
text: Run contrast listening with two minimal pairs before production. Use paced repetition with clear rhythm and immediate corrective model. Keep one process target per session and repeat in short blocks. Record carryover in natural speech at the end.
do_not: Do not skip perception work before production tasks.

title: Phonology Process Intervention Intermediate
text: Target omission patterns with segment blending and full-word production. Add visual markers to support complete syllable output. Structure each session into perception, production, and review. Promote only after two stable sessions.
do_not: Do not replace all training words at once.

title: Phonology Process Intervention Advanced
text: Embed target process correction in story retell and picture description. Use delayed feedback to encourage internal monitoring. Summarize error pattern at session end and set one focus for next visit. Ask family to do short recording and replay practice.
do_not: Do not interrupt every utterance during high-load tasks.

title: Expressive Vocabulary Build Basic
text: Build a topic word set with nouns first, then verbs and attributes. Use picture naming with sentence frames for each word. Give first-sound and semantic cue when retrieval stalls. Recheck old words weekly for retention.
do_not: Do not run naming without contextual use.

title: Expressive Vocabulary Build Intermediate
text: Embed target words into daily-use sentence templates. Use turn-taking questions and require full short-sentence responses. Train fast lexical retrieval on slow-response words. Track mean response latency across sessions.
do_not: Do not correct output without teaching retrieval strategy.

title: Expressive Vocabulary Transfer
text: Use storytelling and rule explanation tasks to force active target-word use. Set a minimum target-word count per task and review after completion. Model paraphrase and synonym substitution for flexibility. Assign themed home conversation practice.
do_not: Do not rely on only yes-no prompts in transfer stage.

title: Semantic Organization Basic
text: Train category sorting first, then feature description for each item. Use six to eight words per set and ask for function or property. Add compare-contrast questions to strengthen semantic links. Finish with one-minute rapid recall.
do_not: Do not keep child in passive repetition mode.

title: Semantic Organization Intermediate
text: Use causal, temporal, and spatial relation prompts. Require keyword planning before full-sentence response. Ask child to explain why when the answer is wrong and then revise. Increase abstraction only after stable 75 percent success.
do_not: Do not skip explanation step after errors.

title: Semantic Organization Narrative Support
text: Use short narrative tasks with person-event-outcome structure. Start with picture ordering and then oral retell. Provide structured feedback on missing semantic elements. Assign one daily event retell at home.
do_not: Do not judge only length; judge information completeness.

title: Nonword Repetition Syllable Hold
text: Start from two-syllable nonwords and expand to longer items. Present once and allow at most one repeat. Mark omission and sequence reversal separately. Increase length only after stable accuracy.
do_not: Do not allow unlimited repetition of the same stimulus.

title: Nonword Repetition Working Memory
text: Use chunking: first half, then full sequence, then delayed repeat. Keep rounds to ten items with clear success target. Revisit incorrect items at end of round. Keep pace controlled to reduce impulsive response.
do_not: Do not reveal full answer before self-correction attempt.

title: Nonword Repetition Transfer
text: Transfer stable nonword patterns to low-frequency real-word learning. Use immediate repeat after auditory input, then delayed repeat. Add light interference task between input and output to train hold. Compare accuracy by syllable length weekly.
do_not: Do not add too many new items in one session.

title: PST Low Score Intervention Basic
text: Use high-frequency scene pictures for key-element naming and description. Follow two steps: word output then sentence output. Review error type right after each block. Prioritize completion stability before complexity.
do_not: Do not skip comprehension check before scoring response.

title: PST Low Score Intervention Intermediate
text: Move to multi-element scenes and require action-relation description. Fade prompts from semantic cue to minimal cue. Keep one theme across a week for trend comparison. Convert frequent errors into next-session warmup.
do_not: Do not overuse the same picture set.

title: PST Low Score Intervention Advanced
text: Use timed scene narration to train extraction speed and structure. Require main event plus two supporting details. Add quick self-check for completeness and logic at the end. Set next-session goal from self-check outcome.
do_not: Do not evaluate by score only; include structure quality.

title: PN Low Score Intervention Basic
text: Teach cause-process-outcome frame with three sequential pictures. Require at least one sentence per stage. Provide transition-word scaffold for coherence. End with one full retell for consolidation.
do_not: Do not chase advanced vocabulary before structure is stable.

title: PN Low Score Intervention Intermediate
text: Expand narrative with emotion, time, and place details. Use checklist prompts to fill missing elements. Redirect repeated or off-topic output quickly. Track completeness and coherence across sessions.
do_not: Do not request all details at once.

title: PN Low Score Intervention Advanced
text: Train post-retell self-check for opening, conflict, and ending. Use replay of recorded speech to mark unclear jumps. Therapist gives only key feedback points and asks child-led revision. Goal is stable independent retell.
do_not: Do not replace child self-revision with therapist rewriting.

title: Receptive Vocabulary Training
text: Use picture choice to map word meaning to visual referent. Ask for short reason after each choice. Contrast-correct wrong choices within the same topic. Re-test learned words weekly.
do_not: Do not give only right-wrong judgment without semantic cue.

title: Grammar Comprehension Training
text: Start from simple SVO sentence listening and picture matching. Add negation and passive forms step by step. Mark keywords before answer to reduce parsing error. Review missed grammar cues in a summary block.
do_not: Do not jump directly to complex clauses.
